assessment.screening_summary: Patient provided minimal, fragmented verbal fluency responses with no elaboration or narrative structure. Task completion was examiner-dependent and lacked depth or coherence. No clear evidence of dementia, but impairment-like signals suggest need for further clinical evaluation.
evidence_for_risk: Lack of spontaneous elaboration or contextual connection
evidence_against_risk: Patient correctly named several common animal nouns
No signs of severe disorientation or confusion
Task was completed with minimal prompting
alternative_explanations: Low verbal fluency due to task unfamiliarity or anxiety
Temporary cognitive load or fatigue during screening
Language or communication style differences not indicative of impairment
risk_control_suggestions: Repeat verbal fluency task with different categories or longer time limits
Include additional screening measures (e.g., memory recall, orientation)
Observe for consistency across multiple sessions or tasks
assessment.visit_recommendation: Formal cognitive screening recommended; routine follow-up if no further concerns arise.
